EIN.value: The Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a nine-digit number assigned by the IRS for reporting employment taxes and managing tax accounts. It is crucial to use the correct EIN on all tax forms and notifications. If you do not have an EIN, you can apply for one online or through other IRS methods. Always ensure consistency with the EIN used for various forms to avoid potential penalties.

citations.snippet: The EIN is a nine-digit number the IRS issues. The digits are arranged as follows: 00-0000000. It is used to identify the tax accounts of employers and certain others who have no employees.
Always be sure the EIN on the form you file exactly matches the EIN the IRS assigned to your business.
Notify the IRS immediately if you change your business name. Write to the IRS office where you file your returns.
Employer Identification Number: 1635 Understanding Your EIN.

ADDRESS.value: A property address is required for every policy, usually in the form of a street address or a descriptive address. The best approach is to use the street address, which includes the street number, street name, unit or apartment number, city or county name, state, and zip code. If a street address is unavailable, a descriptive address such as a rural route or a legal description may be used.

citations.snippet:
A property address is required for every policy, which is usually a street address but may be a descriptive address: 

• Street Address is the best and preferred method; it includes the street number and street name (Address Line 1), unit…

ACCOUNT.value: Our policies prioritize the protection of privacy and security for our products, users, and customers through investments in default security, responsible data practices, and user-controlled settings. Security programs, in collaboration with privacy officials, establish conditions for account access and management, ensuring proper authorization while considering factors like expiration and disabling of accounts. Access to assets is limited to authorized users and managed per assessed risks, with provisions for training on cybersecurity measures. Our account management includes defining types of accounts allowed, monitoring usage, and ensuring compliance with policies, with a focus on protecting against risks associated with certain account types.

citations.snippet: We make it a priority to protect the privacy and security of our products, users, and customers, even if there are near-term financial consequences.
Where access involves personally identifiable information, security programs collaborate with the senior agency official for privacy to establish the specific conditions...for each account.
Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorized users, services, and hardware and managed commensurate with the assessed risk of unauthorized access.
Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system; Assign account managers.

ROUTING.value: The routing policies prioritize protecting privacy and security of information through several key strategies: 1) **Data Minimization**: Limiting data collection to what is necessary for functional purposes. 2) **On-Device Processing**: Employing processing directly on user devices to enhance privacy and reduce the risk of data exposure. 3) **Transparency and User Choice**: Users are informed about data usage and can make choices regarding their information. 4) **Secure Practices**: Implementation of encryption for data in transit, secure key management, and authentication for network communications using standards like TLS and IPsec. This multi-layered approach ensures that security and privacy practices are integrated into organizational processes and are continuously monitored for effectiveness. Furthermore, organizations assess risks associated with operational activities to strengthen overall security and privacy frameworks.

citations.snippet: Focus: Protecting privacy and security of information through data minimization, on-device processing, transparency and user choice, and security
We make it a priority to protect the privacy and security of our products, users, and customers, even if there are near-term financial consequences...
• Implement secure key and certificate management: Store encryption keys and certificates securely and rotate them at appropriate time intervals...
Discussion: Organizations strategically allocate security and privacy controls in the security and privacy architectures so that adversaries must overcome multiple controls...

SIGNER.value: The term "signer" in the context of security and privacy policies refers to individuals whose signatures are used to authenticate documents and agreements. For instance, an insurer may accept electronic submissions contingent on signature authentication processes. This ensures the integrity and verification of the signer's identity, as highlighted in several documents discussing electronic signature verification measures. Moreover, secure practices are emphasized in the policies that protect user data, suggesting a robust framework around the management and authentication of signers.

citations.snippet: We make it a priority to protect the privacy and security of our products, users, and customers, even if there are near-term financial consequences.
The insurer may accept electronic submissions if their business process includes signature authentication and records receipt dates.
